data.title: Braskem taps entire $1B credit line amid debt review - Bloomberg
data.description:
Braskem (NYSE:BAK) has tapped its entire $1 billion credit facility amid a downturn in Brazil's chemical industry and mounting investor concerns over the troubled company, Bloomberg reported Friday.

The unsecured facility with a syndicate of banks is due to expire late next year, and the company has been in talks to renew the line, trying to avoid having to include collateral, according to the report.

Braskem (NYSE:BAK) took investors by surprise last week when it disclosed that it hired advisors to help prepare a "diagnosis of economic and financial alternatives to optimize its capital structure," which was interpreted as a signal that a debt restructuring was on the way.

Petrobras' (PBR) board has begun strategizing how to position itself if Braskem (BAK) is restructured, including potentially injecting capital into the company, Bloomberg reported earlier this week; Braskem is controlled by Petrobras and Novono.

data.title: LGI Homes closes 354 homes in September
data.description:
• LGI Homes (NASDAQ:LGIH) on Friday announced it has closed 354 homes in September 2025, which includes the closing of 28 currently or previously leased single-family rental homes.
  • For the third quarter 2025, it closed a total of 1,107 homes including the closing of 42 currently or previously leased single-family rental homes.
  • As of September 30, 2025, the company had 141 active selling communities.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: U.S. rig count flat after four straight weekly gains in Baker Hughes survey
data.description:
The number of oil and gas drilling rigs in the U.S. remained steady at 549, Baker Hughes reported Friday, after the count had increased for the previous four weeks for the first time since February.

Oil rigs fell by 2 to 422 while gas rigs gained 1 to 118 and 9 rigs were classified as miscellaneous; the overall U.S. rig count was down 36 rigs, or 6.1% compared to the same time last year.

The number of rigs drilling for oil was 57 below the year-ago level, but rigs directed at natural gas were 16 more than a year ago.

Rigs targeting oil in the Permian Basin fell by 2 to 251, the Eagle Ford lost 1 to 33, and the Williston Basin remained unchanged at 29.

data.title: Uranium Energy announces pricing for a public offering of 15.5M shares
data.description:
• Uranium Energy (NYSE:UEC) announced on Friday a price of $13.15 per share for a public offering of 15.5M shares.
  • The company granted the underwriter a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 2.3M shares of its common stock.
  • The offer is expected to close on October 6, 2025.
  • The company plans to use the proceeds from the offering to accelerate the development of a new uranium refining and conversion facility through its wholly owned subsidiary, United States Uranium Refining & Conversion, as well as for general corporate and working capital purposes.
